name: Hihimanu Sushi
tagline: High-end omakase sushi on Waialae — named for the Hawaiian eagle ray, blending island identity with Japanese craft.
talkStory: Opened by Chef Jordan Keao on Waialae Ave in Kaimuki, the name 'hihimanu' is Hawaiian for eagle ray — a nod to blending Hawaiian identity with serious Edomae-style sushi craft. This is a $100+ omakase experience in a humble neighborhood setting, not a Waikiki hotel, which is exactly what makes it special. The fish is sourced from local auction and flown in from Tsukiji. Reserve well in advance — only a handful of counter seats. Dinner only, closed Mondays.
